description: WasmVM Dynamic Module Loading - Replace the monolithic multicall WASM binary with individually compiled standalone WASM binaries, loaded dynamically on demand via filesystem-based discovery

userStories.title: CommandRegistry path-based resolution and dynamic registration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CommandRegistry.resolve() to handle path-based lookups so brush-shell's PATH resolution works when it sends '/bin/ls' instead of 'ls'.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandRegistry.resolve('/bin/ls') returns the driver registered for 'ls'
CommandRegistry.resolve('/usr/bin/grep') returns the driver registered for 'grep'
CommandRegistry.resolve('ls') still works (direct name lookup unchanged)
Add registerCommand(command, driver) method for single-command dynamic registration
Add populateBinEntry(vfs, command) method to create a single /bin stub file
Unit tests for path-based resolution with various path prefixes
Unit tests for registerCommand and populateBinEntry
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: packages/kernel/src/command-registry.ts. The resolve() method currently does a simple Map.get(command). Add basename extraction: if direct lookup fails and command contains '/', extract basename via command.split('/').pop() and retry. See spec Section 4.1. This fixes a latent bug where brush-shell sends '/bin/ls' via proc_spawn but the registry only stores 'ls'.

userStories.title: On-demand command discovery in kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the kernel to ask drivers to resolve unknown commands before throwing ENOENT, enabling dynamic discovery of commands added after init.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional tryResolve?(command: string): boolean to RuntimeDriver interface in types.ts
kernel.spawnInternal() calls driver.tryResolve(command) for each mounted driver when CommandRegistry.resolve() returns null
If tryResolve returns true, kernel calls commandRegistry.registerCommand() and populateBinEntry() then retries spawn
Drivers without tryResolve are skipped (method is optional)
Test: MockRuntimeDriver with tryResolve discovers a command that was not in initial commands list
Test: tryResolve returning false for all drivers still results in ENOENT
Test: after tryResolve succeeds, subsequent spawns resolve via registry without calling tryResolve again
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Files: packages/kernel/src/types.ts (RuntimeDriver interface), packages/kernel/src/kernel.ts (spawnInternal). The kernel stores mounted drivers in this.drivers array. Loop through them in spawnInternal after registry miss. See spec Section 4.1 and 4.3. Use createTestKernel and MockRuntimeDriver from test helpers.

grep lib dispatches on argv[0]: 'egrep' -> Extended mode, 'fgrep' -> Fixed mode, default -> Basic mode

Binary crate has [[bin]] name = 'grep' and minimal fn main()
Update workspace Cargo.toml to include new crates

userStories.title: ModuleCache with concurrent-compile deduplication
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ModuleCache class that compiles WebAssembly.Module once per binary and deduplicates concurrent compilations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ModuleCache class in packages/runtime/wasmvm/src/ with resolve(binaryPath), invalidate(binaryPath), clear() methods
resolve() returns cached WebAssembly.Module if available
resolve() deduplicates concurrent compilations via pending promise map
Two concurrent resolve() calls for the same binary only trigger one compile
invalidate() removes a specific entry from cache
clear() removes all entries
Unit tests for cache hit, cache miss, concurrent dedup, invalidate, clear
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Refactor WasmVmRuntimeDriver for commandDirs and filesystem scan
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WasmVmRuntimeDriver to discover commands from filesystem directories instead of a hardcoded array, with WASM magic byte validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add isWasmBinary() utility that checks first 4 bytes for WASM magic (0x00 0x61 0x73 0x6D)
Replace WasmVmRuntimeOptions.wasmBinaryPath with commandDirs: string[]
init() scans all commandDirs, validates WASM magic bytes, populates internal command map
First directory containing a command wins (PATH semantics)
Dotfiles and directories are skipped during scan
Non-WASM files are silently skipped
Implement tryResolve(command): sync check of commandDirs for on-demand discovery
Remove WASMVM_COMMANDS hardcoded array — commands derived from filesystem scan
spawn() resolves binary path from internal _commandPaths map
Support deprecated wasmBinaryPath for backwards compatibility with deprecation warning
Tests for filesystem scan, magic byte validation, PATH precedence, dotfile skipping, tryResolve
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Worker pre-compiled module transfer via workerData
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the driver to compile WebAssembly.Module on the main thread and transfer it to workers via structured clone, avoiding per-worker compilation.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkerInitData gains optional wasmModule: WebAssembly.Module field
Driver._launchWorker() compiles module via ModuleCache then passes it in workerData
kernel-worker.ts uses init.wasmModule when present, falls back to readFile+compile if absent
Worker calls WebAssembly.instantiate(wasmModule, imports) directly with pre-compiled module
First spawn of a command compiles on main thread (~50-200ms), subsequent spawns instantiate from cache (~1-5ms)
Test: second spawn of same command is significantly faster than first
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-command permission tiers - type definitions and filesystem enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PermissionTier type definitions integrated into WorkerInitData and filesystem-level enforcement (fd_open, fd_write) at the kernel WASI syscall boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Define PermissionTier type: 'full' | 'read-write' | 'read-only' | 'isolated'
Add permissionTier field to WorkerInitData
WasmVmRuntimeOptions gains permissions: Record<string, PermissionTier> config
Driver passes permission tier to worker based on command name lookup
fd_open in kernel-worker.ts checks permission tier before opening files for writing
fd_write in kernel-worker.ts checks permission tier for write-protected operations
read-only tier blocks all write operations (fd_open with write flags, fd_write to non-stdout/stderr)
isolated tier restricts fd_open reads to cwd subtree only
Default unknown/third-party commands to 'read-write'
Test: read-only command cannot write files
Test: isolated command cannot read files outside cwd
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-command permission tiers - process enforcement and wildcard config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need process-level permission enforcement (proc_spawn blocking) and wildcard pattern matching for flexible permission configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: proc_spawn in kernel-worker.ts checks permission tier before spawning subprocesses
Only 'full' tier allows proc_spawn; read-write, read-only, and isolated tiers block it
Wildcard patterns supported in permissions config ('*' for default, '_untrusted/*' for directory prefix)
Pattern matching priority: exact name match first, then longest glob pattern, then '*' fallback
Default first-party commands to appropriate tiers (sh: full, grep: read-only, etc.)
Test: full-tier command can spawn subprocesses
Test: read-write command cannot spawn subprocesses
Test: wildcard pattern '_untrusted/*' matches commands in that directory prefix
Test: exact match takes precedence over wildcard
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: wasi-libc patches - pipe, dup, dup2
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a wasi-libc patch that replaces the ENOSYS stubs for pipe(), dup(), and dup2() with calls to our host_process WASM imports.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/patches/wasi-libc/0001-pipe-dup.patch
Patch adds a new .c file to wasi-libc's libc-bottom-half/sources/
pipe() calls __host_fd_pipe() from host_process.fd_pipe, returns read/write fds
dup() calls __host_fd_dup() from host_process.fd_dup, returns new fd
dup2() calls __host_fd_dup2() from host_process.fd_dup2, returns new fd
Import signatures match wasmvm/crates/wasi-ext/src/lib.rs exactly (i32 params, errno return)
Uses WASM_IMPORT macro pattern from spec Section 2.3
Patch applies cleanly to vendored wasi-libc via patch-wasi-libc.sh --check
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: wasi-libc patches - spawn, wait, kill, getpid
userStories.description: As a developer, I need wasi-libc patches that replace ENOSYS stubs for posix_spawn(), waitpid(), kill(), getpid(), and getppid() with calls to our host_process imports.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/patches/wasi-libc/0002-spawn-wait.patch for posix_spawn() and waitpid()
Create wasmvm/patches/wasi-libc/0003-kill.patch for kill()
Create wasmvm/patches/wasi-libc/0004-getpid.patch for getpid() and getppid()
posix_spawn() calls __host_proc_spawn() from host_process.proc_spawn
posix_spawn() serializes argv as null-terminated strings concatenated in a contiguous buffer, matching wasi-ext proc_spawn ABI
posix_spawn() maps file_actions to stdin_fd/stdout_fd/stderr_fd params; attrp is ignored
waitpid() calls __host_proc_waitpid() from host_process.proc_waitpid
kill() calls __host_proc_kill() from host_process.proc_kill
getpid() calls __host_proc_getpid() (replaces hardcoded 42 in vanilla wasi-libc)
getppid() calls __host_proc_getppid() from host_process.proc_getppid
All import signatures match wasmvm/crates/wasi-ext/src/lib.rs
All patches apply cleanly via patch-wasi-libc.sh --check
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

Create wasmvm/patches/wasi-libc/0006-isatty.patch for isatty
getuid() calls __host_getuid() from host_user.getuid
getgid() calls __host_getgid() from host_user.getgid
geteuid() calls __host_geteuid() from host_user.geteuid
getegid() calls __host_getegid() from host_user.getegid
isatty() calls __host_isatty() from host_user.isatty (replaces basic filetype check)
All import signatures match wasmvm/crates/wasi-ext/src/lib.rs

Add patched sysroot build step to CI (cached, keyed on wasi-libc commit + patch checksums)
Add C test fixture compilation step
Add C parity test execution to CI test matrix
All 26 C test fixtures compile and pass parity in CI
Both native compiler (gcc/clang) and wasi-sdk must be available in CI
Update docs/compatibility-matrix.md with any opted-in C commands
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Comprehensive node binary integration test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a comprehensive test file covering all node CLI behaviors through the kernel so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/secure-exec/tests/kernel/node-binary-behavior.test.ts
Test: node -e 'console.log("hello")' -> stdout contains 'hello', exitCode 0
Test: node -e 'process.exit(42)' -> exitCode 42
Test: node -e 'console.error("err")' -> stderr contains 'err'
Test: node -e 'syntax error' -> exitCode non-zero, stderr contains error name
Test: node -e 'throw new Error("boom")' -> exitCode 1, stderr contains 'boom'
Test: node -e 'setTimeout(()=>console.log("delayed"),100)' -> stdout contains 'delayed'
Test: node -e with process.stdin (reads from stdin pipe) -> works when stdin provided
Test: node -e 'require("fs").readdirSync("/")' -> returns VFS root listing
Test: node -e 'require("child_process").execSync("echo sub").toString()' -> stdout contains 'sub' (cross-runtime)
Test: node --version -> stdout matches semver pattern
Test: node with no args and closed stdin -> exits cleanly (does not hang)
All tests run through kernel.exec() (non-PTY path) AND at least stdout/error tests also verified through interactive shell (PTY path via TerminalHarness)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Fix Ctrl+C at shell prompt not resetting the input line
userStories.description: As a user, I need Ctrl+C at the shell prompt to discard the current partial input, echo ^C, and show a fresh prompt — matching POSIX bash behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Interactive shell: type 'partial command' then Ctrl+C -> '^C' appears, current input discarded, fresh prompt on new line
Interactive shell: empty prompt + Ctrl+C -> '^C' appears, fresh prompt on new line (no error)
Interactive shell: Ctrl+C at prompt does NOT kill the shell process (shell must survive)
Test: TerminalHarness types partial text, sends 0x03, verifies ^C echo and fresh prompt within 1 second
Test: after Ctrl+C at prompt, shell accepts and executes the next command normally
Fix approach: intercept SIGINT at PTY/kernel level when foreground pgid is the shell's own pgid — echo ^C\r\n and re-trigger prompt instead of calling driver.kill() which terminates the WASM worker
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Fix fdPwrite permission bypass and gate proc_kill, fd_pipe, fd_dup by tier
userStories.description: As a developer, I need write/process permission enforcement applied consistently to ALL write and process syscalls so restricted tiers cannot bypass protections.
acceptanceCriteria: Add isWriteBlocked() check to fdPwrite in kernel-worker.ts — mirror the check in fdWrite (allow only fd 1 and 2)
Gate proc_kill with permission check — only 'full' tier can send signals (use isSpawnBlocked() or new isKillBlocked())
Gate fd_pipe with isSpawnBlocked() — pipes are only useful with proc_spawn, restrict to 'full' tier
Gate fd_dup with isSpawnBlocked() — prevent resource exhaustion from restricted tiers
Test: read-only command calling fd_pwrite returns EACCES
Test: read-only command calling proc_kill returns EACCES
Test: isolated command calling fd_pipe returns EACCES
Test: full-tier command can still use fdPwrite, proc_kill, fd_pipe, fd_dup normally
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: SECURITY: fdPwrite bypass is the highest-priority fix. A read-only command can currently write to files via fd_pwrite. File: packages/runtime/wasmvm/src/kernel-worker.ts. fdWrite (line ~163) has the check, fdPwrite (line ~232) does not. proc_kill (line ~540) and fd_pipe/fd_dup (line ~551-601) are also ungated.

userStories.title: Implement proc_getppid and fd_dup2 host imports in kernel-worker and driver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the missing host_process.proc_getppid and host_process.fd_dup2 imports implemented so C/Rust binaries calling getppid() or dup2() don't trap.
acceptanceCriteria: Add proc_getppid function to createHostProcessImports in kernel-worker.ts — writes init.ppid to return pointer
Add fd_dup2 function to createHostProcessImports in kernel-worker.ts — calls fdTable.dup2(oldFd, newFd) via RPC
Add fdDup2 case to driver.ts _handleSyscall RPC dispatcher
Test: WASM binary calling getppid() returns a valid PID (not trap, not 0)
Test: WASM binary calling dup2(stdout, 10) can write to fd 10
Test: dup_test.c parity test passes (previously skipped due to missing fd_dup2)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Both imports are declared in wasmvm/crates/wasi-ext/src/lib.rs and in the C patches (0001-pipe-dup.patch, 0004-getpid.patch) but NOT provided by the JS host. Any WASM binary that links getppid() or dup2() will fail with a WebAssembly.LinkError. WorkerInitData already carries ppid. File: packages/runtime/wasmvm/src/kernel-worker.ts createHostProcessImports().

userStories.title: Gate isolated-tier VFS operations and add integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the isolated permission tier to properly restrict VFS reads to cwd only, with integration tests proving enforcement.
acceptanceCriteria: Add isPathInCwd check to vfs.exists() in kernel-worker.ts VFS proxy — return false for paths outside cwd when isolated
Add isPathInCwd check to vfs.readlink() in kernel-worker.ts — throw VfsError for paths outside cwd when isolated
Add isPathInCwd check to getIno()/resolveIno() in kernel-worker.ts — return null for paths outside cwd when isolated
Add isPathInCwd check to populateDirEntries() in kernel-worker.ts — skip for paths outside cwd when isolated
Integration test: isolated command cannot stat /etc/passwd (path outside cwd)
Integration test: isolated command cannot readdir / (root listing blocked)
Integration test: isolated command CAN read files within its cwd
Integration test: isolated command cannot write files anywhere
Integration test: isolated command cannot spawn subprocesses
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently vfs.exists(), readlink(), getIno() and populateDirEntries() in kernel-worker.ts have zero permission checks for isolated tier. An isolated command can probe the entire VFS structure. The driver.test.ts permission tests (lines ~957-1029) only test read-only, read-write, and full — zero tests for isolated tier.

userStories.title: Move FDTable out of test path and fix module compile error handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need production code to not import from test directories, and module compilation failures to fail fast instead of silently launching broken workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Move FDTable from packages/runtime/wasmvm/test/helpers/test-fd-table.ts to packages/runtime/wasmvm/src/fd-table.ts
Update kernel-worker.ts import to use the new src/ path
Update test imports to use the new src/ path
Replace empty catch {} in driver.ts _launchWorker ModuleCache.resolve() with proper error propagation
If module compilation fails, throw immediately with descriptive error instead of launching worker with undefined module
Test: spawning a command with a corrupt WASM binary produces a clear error (not silent failure)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: kernel-worker.ts line 18 imports FDTable from ../test/helpers/test-fd-table.ts — production code importing from test path. driver.ts lines ~450-456 has empty catch {} that swallows module compilation errors. Both are reliability/deployment hazards.

userStories.title: Fix isPathInCwd symlink escape and validate unknown tier strings
userStories.description: As a developer, I need isPathInCwd to resolve symlinks before checking the path prefix, and unknown permission tier strings to default to the most restrictive tier.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix isPathInCwd in permission-check.ts to use realpath (resolve symlinks) instead of normalize(resolve())
An isolated command cannot read /cwd/symlink-to-etc/passwd even if the symlink is inside cwd
Add runtime validation for PermissionTier in kernel-worker.ts — if tier is not one of 'full'|'read-write'|'read-only'|'isolated', default to 'isolated'
Test: symlink inside cwd pointing outside cwd is blocked for isolated tier
Test: unknown tier string 'admin' defaults to isolated behavior (blocks writes and spawns)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: isPathInCwd in permission-check.ts (lines ~22-26) uses normalize(resolve()) which is purely lexical — no symlink resolution. A symlink inside cwd pointing to /etc would pass the prefix check. Unknown tier strings fall through inconsistently: isWriteBlocked returns false (allows writes!) but isSpawnBlocked returns true.

userStories.title: Fix vfsReadFile SAB overflow guard and lstat fallthrough
userStories.description: As a developer, I need vfsReadFile to check buffer bounds before writing, and lstat to not follow symlinks.
acceptanceCriteria: In driver.ts _handleSyscall vfsReadFile case: check content.length against DATA_BUFFER_BYTES BEFORE calling data.set(content, 0)
If content exceeds buffer, return EIO immediately (not via catch-block RangeError conversion)
Apply same pre-check to vfsReaddir and vfsStat cases in _handleSyscall
Fix lstat() in kernel-worker.ts VFS proxy to not follow symlinks (currently falls through to stat())
Test: reading a file >1MB from VFS returns EIO cleanly (not RangeError crash)
Test: lstat on a symlink returns the symlink's metadata, not the target's
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: driver.ts vfsReadFile (line ~746) calls data.set(content, 0) unconditionally before the overflow guard at line ~774. If content >1MB, data.set() throws RangeError which the catch block converts to EIO — fragile. Contrast with fdRead/fdPread which correctly check size BEFORE data.set(). kernel-worker.ts lstat (line ~393) just calls this.stat(path) — symlinks always followed.

userStories.title: Fix posix_spawn file_actions and waitpid return value
userStories.description: As a developer, I need posix_spawn to handle addclose/addopen file_actions and waitpid to return the actual child PID.
acceptanceCriteria: Update 0002-spawn-wait.patch: posix_spawn iteration over file_actions now processes OP_CLOSE and OP_OPEN ops (not just dup2)
For OP_CLOSE: call __wasi_fd_close() on the specified fd before spawning
For OP_OPEN: open the file and dup2 it to the target fd before spawning
Fix waitpid return value: when pid > 0, return the actual waited-for PID from the host (not just echo back the input pid)
waitpid(-1, ...) returns the PID of any exited child (not -1)
Test: posix_spawn with addclose correctly closes pipe ends in child
Test: waitpid returns correct child PID
Rebuild patched sysroot after patch update
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: 0002-spawn-wait.patch line ~246: posix_spawn iteration only extracts dup2 ops targeting fds 0/1/2 for stdin_fd/stdout_fd/stderr_fd. addclose and addopen are silently ignored. waitpid (line ~289) always returns the input pid instead of the actual waited-for PID. Also __addfdop uses prepend (not append) so action execution order is reversed.

userStories.title: C parity test improvements - stderr, sleep_test tier, getpid assertions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the C parity tests to compare stderr, fix sleep_test classification, and strengthen getpid_test assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add stderr comparison to all Tier 2+ parity tests in c-parity.test.ts (currently only stdout and exit code are compared)
Move sleep_test out of PATCHED_PROGRAMS list in Makefile — it uses vanilla nanosleep(), not host_process.sleep_ms
Add isatty_test to PATCHED_PROGRAMS list in Makefile (it uses host_user.isatty from patched sysroot)
Strengthen getpid_test parity: verify WASM PID is > 0, != 42, and consistent with kernel-assigned PID (check via a second getpid call returning same value)
Strengthen kill_child parity: add WIFSIGNALED check or verify child exit status indicates signal termination
Strengthen userinfo parity: verify WASM uid/gid values are specific (e.g., 1000) not just format-matching
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: c-parity.test.ts Tier 2+ tests only compare stdout and exitCode, never stderr. sleep_test uses vanilla nanosleep() not host_process.sleep_ms — mislabeled as Tier 2. isatty_test IS missing from PATCHED_PROGRAMS in Makefile line 69. getpid_test passes with any positive non-42 value. kill_child never checks WIFSIGNALED. userinfo just checks format regex.

userStories.title: C syscall coverage integration test
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single comprehensive C program that exercises every libc-to-WASI and libc-to-host-import syscall path, proving end-to-end coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/c/programs/syscall_coverage.c that tests ALL syscall categories in one program
WASI FD ops: open, read, write, pread, pwrite, seek, close, fstat, ftruncate
WASI path ops: mkdir, stat, rename, unlink, rmdir, opendir/readdir/closedir, symlink, readlink
WASI args/env/clock: argc/argv, environ, clock_gettime (CLOCK_REALTIME and CLOCK_MONOTONIC)
host_process: pipe, dup, dup2, getpid, getppid, posix_spawn+waitpid, kill
host_user: getuid, getgid, geteuid, getegid, isatty
Each syscall prints a structured line: 'syscall_name: ok' or 'syscall_name: FAIL (reason)'
Program exits 0 only if ALL syscalls succeed
Add parity test in c-parity.test.ts: native output === WASM output
Add to PATCHED_PROGRAMS in Makefile (requires patched sysroot)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the capstone integration test. It exercises the full C -> libc -> WASI/host_import -> JS host -> kernel path for every syscall we provide. The structured output format makes it easy to identify which specific syscall fails. Depends on US-038 (proc_getppid and fd_dup2 must be implemented first). See the canonical import lists: wasi-ext/src/lib.rs for host_process/host_user, wasi-polyfill.ts for wasi_snapshot_preview1.

userStories.title: Fix spawn RPC data race in driver.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the spawn RPC response to use proper synchronization so the child exit code doesn't race with subsequent RPC calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix managed.wait().then() in driver.ts _handleSyscall spawn case to not write to dataBuf without Atomics notification
Child exit code must be delivered through a mechanism that doesn't race with the worker's next RPC call
Option A: use a separate SharedArrayBuffer slot for async spawn results
Option B: make spawn synchronous by blocking until child exits (simpler but limits parallelism)
Test: rapid spawn/wait cycles don't produce incorrect exit codes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: driver.ts lines ~664-667: after kernel.spawn(), managed.wait().then((code) => { view.setInt32(0, code, true); }) writes to the shared data buffer asynchronously. By the time the child exits, the worker may have issued another RPC call using the same dataBuf, causing data corruption. The write has no Atomics.notify() to wake the worker.

userStories.title: Fix poll_oneoff clock subscription sleep behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I need poll_oneoff to actually block for clock subscriptions so nanosleep()/sleep() work correctly from C programs.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix poll_oneoff in wasi-polyfill.ts: clock subscriptions must block for the requested duration (not return immediately)
Use Atomics.wait() or host_process.sleep_ms for the blocking delay
Relative clock subscriptions (CLOCK_MONOTONIC) sleep for the specified timeout_ns
Absolute clock subscriptions (CLOCK_REALTIME) sleep until the specified wallclock time
Test: C program calling nanosleep(50ms) actually takes >= 40ms (80% of requested time)
Test: poll_oneoff with zero timeout returns immediately
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: wasi-polyfill.ts lines ~1303-1306: clock subscriptions are completed immediately without any delay. nanosleep() from C goes through poll_oneoff and returns instantly. The host_process.sleep_ms exists and blocks via Atomics.wait but poll_oneoff doesn't use it. This means any C program using standard sleep/nanosleep/usleep gets zero delay.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md with WasmVM syscall coverage policy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CLAUDE.md updated with policies ensuring every host import has test coverage and new imports cannot be added without tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'WasmVM Syscall Coverage' section to CLAUDE.md
Document: every function in host_process and host_user import modules must have at least one C parity test exercising it through libc
Document: when adding a new host import, add a matching test case to syscall_coverage.c and its parity test
Document: the canonical source of truth for import signatures is wasmvm/crates/wasi-ext/src/lib.rs — C patches and JS host must match exactly
Document: C patches in patches/wasi-libc/ must be kept in sync with wasi-ext — ABI drift between C, Rust, and JS is a P0 bug
Document: permission tier enforcement must cover ALL write/spawn/kill/pipe operations — audit kernel-worker.ts when adding new syscalls
Document: PATCHED_PROGRAMS in wasmvm/c/Makefile must include all programs that use host_process or host_user imports
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This codifies the lessons from the adversarial review: missing host imports (proc_getppid, fd_dup2), permission bypass (fdPwrite), and ABI drift between C/Rust/JS are all bugs that happened because there was no policy requiring coverage. File: /home/nathan/secure-exec-1/CLAUDE.md under the existing 'WASM Binary' section.

userStories.title: Add getpwuid C patch, test program, and syscall_coverage entry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need getpwuid() accessible from C programs via a wasi-libc patch, with a dedicated test and syscall_coverage.c entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/patches/wasi-libc/0007-getpwuid.patch exposing __host_getpwuid from host_user.getpwuid
C wrapper: struct passwd *getpwuid(uid_t uid) — calls __host_getpwuid, parses returned 'name:x:uid:gid:gecos:home:shell' string into struct passwd
Import signature matches wasmvm/crates/wasi-ext/src/lib.rs line 125 exactly (uid, buf_ptr, buf_len, ret_len params)
Create wasmvm/c/programs/getpwuid_test.c — calls getpwuid(1000), prints pw_name, pw_uid, pw_gid, pw_dir, pw_shell
getpwuid_test.c validates returned uid matches requested uid
getpwuid_test.c validates pw_name is non-empty and pw_dir is non-empty
Add getpwuid_test to PATCHED_PROGRAMS in wasmvm/c/Makefile
Add getpwuid test to syscall_coverage.c host_user section — call getpwuid(1000), validate pw_name non-null and pw_uid == 1000
Add 'getpwuid' to expectedSyscalls list in c-parity.test.ts
Add parity test for getpwuid_test in c-parity.test.ts
Rebuild patched sysroot after adding patch
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: getpwuid is declared in wasi-ext/src/lib.rs:125, implemented in JS (user.ts:146-175), but has NO C patch — C programs can't call it. The JS impl returns 'user1000:x:1000:1000::/home/user:/bin/sh' format. Follow 0005-user-identity.patch structure for the patch. The struct passwd definition exists in wasi-libc's pwd.h but getpwuid() is stubbed to return NULL.

userStories.title: Fix vacuous host_user assertions in syscall_coverage.c
userStories.description: As a developer, I need syscall_coverage.c to validate return values from getuid/getgid/geteuid/getegid instead of discarding them with (void).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace (void)getuid(); OK('getuid') with: uid_t u = getuid(); TEST('getuid', u == 1000, 'expected 1000')
Replace (void)getgid(); OK('getgid') with: gid_t g = getgid(); TEST('getgid', g == 1000, 'expected 1000')
Replace (void)geteuid(); OK('geteuid') with: uid_t eu = geteuid(); TEST('geteuid', eu == 1000, 'expected 1000')
Replace (void)getegid(); OK('getegid') with: gid_t eg = getegid(); TEST('getegid', eg == 1000, 'expected 1000')
Add isatty validation: TEST('isatty_stdin', isatty(0) == 0, 'stdin should not be tty when piped')
Native parity: native build also returns uid/gid from the OS — update parity test to handle differing uid values between native and WASM
Option A: skip exact stdout match for host_user section and validate WASM assertions independently
Option B: run native as uid 1000 if possible, or normalize uid values in parity comparison
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: syscall_coverage.c lines 321-324 discard return values with (void) cast — a broken implementation returning garbage would pass. Compare to getpid_test.c which validates pid > 0, pid != 42, and consistency. The challenge is that native getuid() returns the host user's real UID (e.g., 1000 on Linux, 501 on macOS) while WASM always returns 1000. The parity test currently does exact stdout comparison (line 504), so the test approach needs to handle this divergence.

userStories.title: Add getppid parent-child verification test
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test proving getppid() returns the actual parent's PID, not just any positive number.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/c/programs/getppid_verify.c — parent calls getpid(), spawns child that calls getppid(), child prints both values
Child program prints: parent_pid=N and my_ppid=N, plus match=yes/no
Verify child's getppid() equals parent's getpid()
Add getppid_verify to PATCHED_PROGRAMS in wasmvm/c/Makefile
Add parity test in c-parity.test.ts — verify match=yes in both native and WASM output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Current getppid_test.c only checks ppid > 0 — a hardcoded 'return 1' would pass. This test spawns a child that verifies its ppid matches the parent's pid, proving the kernel correctly tracks parent-child relationships across proc_spawn. The child program can be a simple C program that prints getppid(), or the parent can spawn 'sh -c' with a command that reads getppid.

userStories.title: Add SIGKILL signal test and signal edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need signal delivery tests for SIGKILL (not just SIGTERM) and edge cases like signaling an already-exited process.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/c/programs/signal_tests.c with multiple signal test scenarios
Test 1: spawn child, send SIGKILL (9), verify WIFSIGNALED and WTERMSIG==9
Test 2: spawn child, let it exit normally, then call kill() on the exited PID — verify kill returns -1 with errno ESRCH (or 0 per POSIX for recently-exited)
Test 3: call kill() with invalid PID (99999) — verify returns -1
Each test prints structured output: 'test_name: ok' or 'test_name: FAIL (reason)'
Add signal_tests to PATCHED_PROGRAMS in wasmvm/c/Makefile
Add parity test in c-parity.test.ts
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: kill_child.c only tests SIGTERM (15). SIGKILL (9) is the only other signal with real behavior in our architecture (worker.terminate()). Testing signal to exited process verifies zombie handling. Testing invalid PID verifies error handling in proc_kill. The JS handler (kernel-worker.ts:624-628) only checks isSpawnBlocked, not whether the PID exists — kernel.kill() handles the ESRCH error.

userStories.title: Add pipe edge case tests - large writes, broken pipe, EOF
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pipe tests covering buffer limits, write-to-closed-pipe behavior, and proper EOF semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/c/programs/pipe_edge.c with multiple pipe edge case tests
Test 1: write >64KB to pipe — verify all data is received (reader must loop until EOF)
Test 2: close read end, write to write end — verify write returns -1 with errno EPIPE (or process receives SIGPIPE)
Test 3: close write end, read from read end — verify read returns 0 (EOF)
Test 4: create pipe, close both ends, verify no leak (process exits cleanly)
Each test prints structured output with ok/FAIL
Add pipe_edge to PATCHED_PROGRAMS in wasmvm/c/Makefile
Add parity test in c-parity.test.ts
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: pipe_test.c writes only 18 bytes. The kernel pipe buffer is 64KB (MAX_PIPE_BUFFER_BYTES). Writing >64KB tests buffer pressure and blocking behavior. Write-to-closed-pipe tests EPIPE/SIGPIPE — our kernel throws EPIPE but doesn't send SIGPIPE (known gap in posix-compatibility.md). Test 3 verifies EOF semantics. Use a helper child process or fork-like pattern for test 2 (need two processes sharing the pipe).

userStories.title: Extract shared posix_spawn_file_actions_t header
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the posix_spawn_file_actions_t typedef in a shared header instead of duplicated across 6 C programs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/c/include/posix_spawn_compat.h with the posix_spawn_file_actions_t typedef and related types
Include posix_spawn_file_actions_adddup2, posix_spawn_file_actions_addclose, posix_spawn_file_actions_init, posix_spawn_file_actions_destroy declarations
Include posix_spawnattr_t typedef
Update spawn_child.c to #include "posix_spawn_compat.h" and remove inline typedef
Update kill_child.c to use shared header
Update waitpid_return.c to use shared header
Update pipeline.c to use shared header
Update spawn_exit_code.c to use shared header
Update syscall_coverage.c to use shared header
Update wasmvm/c/Makefile to add -I include/ to CFLAGS for all PATCHED_PROGRAMS
Verify all 6 programs still compile and pass parity tests
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The identical typedef struct { int __pad0[2]; void *__actions; int __pad[16]; } posix_spawn_file_actions_t; is copy-pasted in 6 C files. If the patched wasi-libc definition changes, all 6 must be updated manually. A shared header eliminates this risk. The -I include/ flag in CFLAGS makes the header available to all programs.

userStories.title: Fix populateBinEntry async race in kernel spawnInternal
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the populateBinEntry() call in kernel.ts spawnInternal to be properly awaited so /bin stubs are created before brush-shell PATH lookup.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix kernel.ts spawnInternal: await the populateBinEntry() call when tryResolve succeeds
If spawnInternal is not already async, make the tryResolve+register+populate path async
Alternative: make populateBinEntry synchronous if VFS.writeFile is sync in the SimpleVFS implementation
Test: on-demand discovery creates /bin stub before subsequent spawn resolves via PATH
Test: rapid consecutive spawns of a newly-discovered command both succeed (no race)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: kernel.ts line 404: populateBinEntry() is declared async (returns Promise<void>) but called without await in spawnInternal(). The /bin stub may not exist when brush-shell tries PATH lookup on subsequent invocations. This is a race condition — it works most of the time because the VFS write is fast, but is not guaranteed. Check if spawnInternal is already async (it likely is since it calls spawn which is async). If so, just add await.

userStories.title: Add waitpid edge case tests - concurrent children and invalid PID
userStories.description: As a developer, I need waitpid tests covering multiple concurrent children with ordering, and error cases for invalid PIDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wasmvm/c/programs/waitpid_edge.c with multiple waitpid edge case tests
Test 1: spawn 3 children with different exit codes, waitpid each by specific PID — verify correct exit code for each
Test 2: spawn 2 children, use wait() (waitpid -1) twice — verify both children reaped (returned PIDs are valid and distinct)
Test 3: waitpid with PID that was never spawned — verify returns -1 with errno ECHILD
Each test prints structured output with ok/FAIL
Add waitpid_edge to PATCHED_PROGRAMS in wasmvm/c/Makefile
Add parity test in c-parity.test.ts
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: waitpid_return.c only tests sequential single-child scenarios. Test 1 exercises the kernel's ability to track multiple concurrent children. Test 2 verifies wait() (-1) returns valid PIDs when multiple children have exited. Test 3 verifies ECHILD error for invalid PID. The JS handler (kernel-worker.ts:604-608) tracks children in a Set — test 2 verifies Set iteration order is safe.
